india_trends.summary: Walmart-owned Flipkart is set to enter India's burgeoning movie and concert ticket market, aiming for a May launch. india_trends.summary: Rising oil prices could push India's current account deficit to a concerning two percent of GDP, as highlighted by a Crisil report. india_trends.title: Bajaj Consumer Care shares soar 16% after Q4 net profit doubles to Rs 63 crore
india_trends.summary: Bajaj Consumer Care shares surged significantly on Friday. The company reported a substantial increase in its net profit, more than doubling compared to the previous year. india_trends.title: India seen as stable global capital hub with $4.4 trillion market cap: SEBI chief
india_trends.summary: India is increasingly recognized as a stable global capital hub with its market capitalization reaching USD 4.4 trillion and a total of USD 154 billion raised through equity and debt markets in FY26, according to Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey.

india_trends.title: Windlas Biotech announces Rs 47 crore buyback at Rs 1,000/share; sets April 24 as record date
india_trends.summary: Windlas Biotech approved a Rs 47 crore share buyback at Rs 1,000 per share via the tender route, with April 24 set as the record date. The move covers 2.23% equity and excludes promoters. Shares rose 2% following the announcement, reflecting positive investor sentiment toward the capital return plan

india_trends.title: Top Gainers & Losers on April 17: Angel One, Triveni Turbine, Zen Tech, Emami, OLA, Suzlon Energy among top gainers
india_trends.summary: Indian stocks gained strength on April 17, with key indices rising over 0.50%, supported by consumer goods and oil &amp; gas sectors. The Nifty 50 and S&amp;P BSE Sensex closed higher, while broader markets showed even stronger gains amid easing tensions in the Middle East.

india_trends.title: Sensex gains 500 points, Nifty 50 ends above 24,350; investors earn ₹5 lakh crore in a day

india_trends.title: Banks' gross NPA's to stay range-bound at 2.0-2.2 per cent by March 2027 despite West Asia risks: Crisil
india_trends.summary: Indian banks are likely to keep asset quality under control over the next two years, with gross non-performing assets (NPAs) projected at 2.0-2.2 per cent by March 2027, only a shade higher than the estimated historic low of 2.0 per cent as of March 2026, according to ratingas agency Crisil Ratings.

india_trends.title: Expert view: SAMCO CEO Jimeet Modi doesn't expect a runway rally on D-Street, sees market consolidating through 2026
india_trends.summary: Jimeet Modi, CEO of SAMCO Group, offers a cautious perspective on the Indian stock market, anticipating a period of consolidation rather than a runaway rally through 2026. He believes the Q4 earnings season may remain muted.
